trivia_bullets.text: Vexing Scuttler is typed as Eldrazi Crab, a creature class that represents Emrakul's corruption of Innistrad's native fauna rather than a true Eldrazi spawn. The Eldritch Moon set design concept, per Mark Rosewater's Making Magic column, was that Emrakul does not create new Eldrazi but mutates existing creatures into her image. [Wizards.com: 'Over the Moon, Part 2', 2016-07-04]

The emerge mechanic, which Vexing Scuttler carries, was designed by Mark Rosewater and is explicitly inspired by the ninjutsu mechanic from Betrayers of Kamigawa. The sacrifice-then-emerge structure was intended to evoke an 'Alien chestburster feel' per Rosewater's own description in his design column. The colored mana component of the emerge cost cannot be reduced by the sacrificed creature's mana value, keeping emerge creatures anchored to their color identity. [Wizards.com: 'Over the Moon, Part 2', 2016-07-04]

Vexing Scuttler has an EDHREC rank of 16,726 [Scryfall], indicating low Commander adoption. Its emerge cost of {6}{U} combined with a base cost of {8} makes it a 4/5 that recurs one instant or sorcery, which is evaluated as underpowered relative to Commander card density standards. Pioneer and Modern legal. [Scryfall]

James Paick is the sole artist on this card. Scryfall records only one printing of Vexing Scuttler (Eldritch Moon no. 11), making this a non-reprinted card. Paick has illustrated approximately 345 cards for Magic: The Gathering and also works as a concept artist for video games and film. [artofmtg.com: James Paick artist page; Scryfall prints search]
